MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC.'s Motion For Sanctions is continued to May 29, 2014 per the request of the parties. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Tyree P. Jones, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP, Tel. 202.414.9296, for defendants and moving party, by Court Call;
Jason Erlich, Esq. of McCormack & Erlich, Tel. 415.296.8420, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC.'S and BAE SYSTEMS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Granted as to Defendants BAE Systems San Francisco Ship Repair Inc. ("SFSR") and BAE Systems, Inc.'s ("BAE") motion for summary judgment. BAE has met its initial burden of proof to show that no employment relationship existed between Plaintiff and BAE. (Laird v. Capital Cities/ABC, Inc. (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 727, 737, disagreed with on another ground in Reid v. Google, Inc. (2010) 50 Cal.4th 512, 524. See UMF nos. 9, 42.) In turn, Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden to show a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff's belief that he was employed by both Defendants is insufficient to create a triable issue of fact. (Salas Decl., 3.) Notwithstanding whether Plaintiff suffered from a disability, SFSR has met its initial burden of proof to show that Plaintiff's disability was not known to SFSR. "An adverse employment decision cannot be made 'because of' a disability, when the disability is not known to the employer.' [Citations.]" (Avila v. Cont'l Airlines, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1237, 1247.) Although knowledge of the disability can be inferred, "knowledge will only be imputed to the employer when the fact of disability is the only reasonable interpretation of the known facts. 'Vague or conclusory statements revealing an unspecified incapacity are not sufficient . . . .' " (Id. at p. 1248.) Given the circumstances, Plaintiff's statement to SFSR at the random drug test is conclusory. (UMF no. 12.) In turn, Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden to demonstrate a triable issue of fact. In the Visit Verification, Dr. Ni did not report that Plaintiff suffered from a disability; Dr. Ni stated the reasons for the visit. (See Salas Decl., Ex. 1.) Dr. Gasparini's deposition does not support Plaintiff's argument. Plaintiff did not discuss any daytime urinary problems. (Plaintiff's Evid., Ex. 5, p. 8:15-19. See Salas Decl., Ex. 3.) Dr. Gasparini reported that in the daytime, Plaintiff stated he voided fairly well. (Id.) Dr. Gasparini's ruled out benign prostatic hypertrophy ("BPH") because "he mentioned that his symptoms were mainly at nighttime. And daytime is - my history says, 'Daytime, patient voids fairly well.' " (Plaintiff's Evid., Ex. 5, p. 13:20-23.) Dr. Gasparini's physical exam indicated that Plaintiff did not suffer from BPH (Id. at p. 13:24 - 14:6, 36:24 - 37:1.) Plaintiff's reliance on Dr. Fan and Dr. Patel's reports does not create a triable issue of fact. Neither doctor reported that Plaintiff suffered from BPH during the adverse employment decisions on Oct. 18 and 24, 2012. Rather, their reports indicate that Plaintiff suffered from BPH during his medical visits from Nov. 4, 2013 onward. (Salas Decl., Exhibits 6, 7.) The accommodation requested by Plaintiff (UMF no. 19) is unreasonable because it would violate the collective bargaining agreement. (McCullah v. So. Calif. Gas Co. (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 495, 501. See Defendant's Evid., Ex. 23, Art. 23.2, (initial screening of urine specimen).) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order that complies with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.) Judge: A. James Robertson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-17-2014 Continued To Apr-04-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JASON ERLICH, ESQ., 415.296.8420, FOR PLAINTIFF LUIS SALAS.
CRAIG BAUMGARTNER, ESQ., 415.659.5916, FOR DEFENDANTS BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC. AND BAE SYSTEMS, INC.

RULING: MATTER DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling WITH MODIFICATION. DEFENDANTs BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC. and BAE SYSTEMS, INC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is continued to April 4, 2014 PER PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST. (CCP 437c(h).) Counsel's declaration shows that facts essential to justify opposition may exist, but cannot be presented because of the need to take the depositions of Mark Gasparini, Bill Cahill, Percy Randall Wade, and Antonio Moroles. (Erlich Decl., 3 - 51.) Opposition and reply to the motion may be filed in accordance with CCP 437c(b)(2) & (4). The trial date of February 18, 2014 is vacated, and the new trial date is set for JUNE 9, 2014. The discovery cut-off date is not affected by the trial continuance. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Set For Trial On Feb-18-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-16-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JASON M. ERLICH OF THE McCORMACK ERLICH LLP, TEL. NO. 296-8420 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. CRAIG N. BAUMGARTNER OF REED/SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 659-5916 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR, INC. and BAE SYSTEMS, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled. Bae Systems, Inc. is a proper defendant as Mr. Salas alleges that he was employed by both SFSR and Bae Systems, Inc. Further, Mr. Salas has pled sufficient facts to allege cause of action based on FEHA violations. Defendants raise questions of fact not subject to a demurrer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
